item.title: OpenAI proposes handing Trump administration 5% stake
item.description: Sam Altman’s start-up in early talks for a public ownership deal as political pressure rises

item.title: Bank of England to push ahead with plan to limit hedge fund leverage
item.description: Move to make gilt market more resilient has been criticised for potentially raising funding costs

item.title: Bending Spoons takes private equity public
item.description: The Italian tech group behind AOL is a good analogue for PE-backed software and tech roll-ups

item.title: HMRC’s clampdown on the corporate structure that saves City firms millions
item.description: UK tax authority has won a series of legal cases involving limited liability partnerships

item.title: ‘Let it rip’: how US egg producers gamed prices while blaming bird flu
item.description: Evidence amassed by prosecutors shows executives privately colluded to keep prices aloft while shifting focus for cause

item.title: EU weighs weaker data centre climate rules in win for Big Tech
item.description: Past proposals for strict rules on renewable energy certificates have been dropped after heavy lobbying, draft shows

item.title: UBS to trial US banking services in push for wealthy American clients
item.description: Swiss group plans to open accounts for staff as early as December to test pricing and products

item.title: How do we save the wilting new homes market?
item.description: Like it or not, the government will have to help developers before houses and rents become even more unaffordable

item.title: Americans prepare for most expensive July 4 celebration ever
item.description: Tariffs and war in Iran have kept food and gasoline prices high as country marks 250th birthday

item.title: How to lift Europe out of high-tech mediocrity
item.description: Red tape, not funding, is the real bottleneck for competitiveness

item.title: BAE’s next-generation combat jet lifted by defence funding boost
item.description: New technology is speeding up development and cutting costs of military equipment

item.title: Battery start-ups see ‘crazy’ demand to smooth power surges in data centres
item.description: Rapid growth of clusters of processors for AI training drives need for energy storage

item.title: The stablecoin bandwagon is getting crowded
item.description: For all their momentum, stablecoins’ moderate benefits come with a host of drawbacks

item.title: Digital finance could spur Eurozone integration
item.description: Stablecoins could provide a common safe asset for the bloc and boost demand for the euro and bonds

item.title: City on the Edge — can San Francisco’s soul survive techno-capitalism?
item.description: Jonathan Weber takes a fascinating look at how political mismanagement and the pursuit of boundless tech power have changed America’s countercultural heartland

item.title: Argentina angered by prospect of oil boom in Falklands
item.description: Islands’ first commercial production in 2028 would transform tiny fishing-dependent economy

item.title: UBS bets on the US
item.description: The Swiss lender wants to woo wealthy Americans with everyday banking services

item.title: Last bastion for physical media falls as Sony dumps PlayStation discs
item.description: Move is first by a major console maker and sparks angry response from some gamers

item.title: ‘China’s Instagram’ targets male users as RedNote readies for IPO
item.description: Social media platform Xiaohongshu accelerates plans for Hong Kong listing

item.title: Playrix billionaire to fund International Booker Prize
item.description: Daria and Dmitri Bukhman, who founded gaming app, to back award for translated fiction

item.title: White House accelerates plans for AI model standards
item.description: Guidance to be announced as soon as next week after government intervention in Anthropic and OpenAI rollouts

item.title: FirstFT: Megadeals drive record $2.8tn in M&A
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: the Chinese carmakers designing AI chips, and new Fed chair vows ‘no changes’ to US central bank independence

item.title: Mega takeovers drive record $2.8tn in dealmaking
item.description: Companies and investors turn to M&A as they adjust to economic shifts driven by the rise of AI

item.title: KNDS puts its plans for a stock market listing on hold
item.description: Franco-German tank maker said it would wait for ‘more favourable market conditions’

item.title: Italian tech group Bending Spoons jumps on Nasdaq debut
item.description: Acquisitive owner of Vimeo, AOL and Eventbrite raises $1.68bn in one of Europe’s biggest technology IPOs this year

item.title: Zelenskyy urges EU to sanction Russian-owned alumina plant in Ireland
item.description: Call on the opening day of Dublin’s EU presidency adds to pressure over Aughinish Alumina smelter

item.title: Shares in Uber-backed Lime jump after it raised $167mn in IPO
item.description: Bike and scooter group’s shares were initially priced at $25 in deal underwritten by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and Jefferies

item.title: US voters support Trump trade deal with Canada and Mexico, survey finds
item.description: New poll finds bipartisan backing for USMCA even as president seeks to renegotiate its terms

item.title: This is how we can make AI safe for everyone
item.description: The labs develop the technology, but citizens and their elected representatives must make the rules

item.title: Saks’ retail rebirth depends more on Gucci than Wall Street
item.description: Retailer has had to repair not only the balance sheet but also the rift with luxury brands it stocked
